name: Aortic Thromboembolism
description: Aortic thromboembolism (ATE) occurs when a blood clot lodges at the aortic trifurcation, obstructing blood flow to the hindlimbs. While more common in cats, it occurs in dogs with underlying cardiac or hypercoagulable disease, causing acute hindlimb paralysis.
treatment_overview: Emergency treatment with anticoagulants (heparin, clopidogrel), analgesia, and supportive care. Thrombolytics (tissue plasminogen activator) can be used but carry haemorrhage risk. Prognosis is guarded; reoccurrence is common.
prevention_tips: Anticoagulation therapy in high-risk cardiac patients
Early treatment of underlying cardiac disease
